title: trifolium maritimum subsp. nigrocinctum
intro: Trifolium maritimum subsp. nigrocinctum, also known as the Black-banded Sea Clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant has been traditionally used to soothe coughs and relieve symptoms of indigestion due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Herbal preparations of Trifolium maritimum subsp. nigrocinctum can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to promote overall health and well-being.
